name: Bryce Young
headline: Career highs, but the efficiency still says backup
body:
Young set personal bests in 2025 with 23 touchdowns, 3,011 yards and a 63.6% completion rate across 16 games, yet the underlying profile barely budged. His EPA per play sat at -0.03 for the second straight year, his success rate ticked up only to 42.6%, and a finish as our QB21 tells you the volume never arrived. A 7.7% deep-attempt rate and 4.8 completed air yards per completion confirm an offense that lived underneath, and 11 picks against modest production cap the ceiling.

There are flickers of growth: CPOE climbed to +1.0, the bad-throw rate fell to 16.4%, and on-target percentage rose to 77.9%. But 216 rushing yards and two scores add little, and the half-season of sub-25-attempt games signals a coaching staff still managing him. Treat Young as a superflex-only stash with real bust risk, not a one-quarterback option.
